summary: One surgical edit: restored stove-light detail in the 2 AM kitchen scene (line 183) to maintain continuity with chapter 7's nighttime light inventory, where the stove light is established as a persistent ambient element. Entry and exit seams are otherwise strong — thematic threads (refrigerator clicks, separate sleeping, spatial awareness, door-ajar detail) bridge cleanly to chapter 7, and detail consistency (Theo's cereal/headphones/sketchbook, Mira's 6:15 time marker) bridges cleanly to chapter 9. Flagged one cross-chapter discrepancy for resolution in chapter 7's seam pass: chapter 7 describes 'the inch of open door' while chapter 8 establishes 'four inches' as a precisely measured, thematically embedded detail (three occurrences plus the thumb-measuring characterization). Chapter 8's measurement should be treated as authoritative. Opening construction ('The shower ran at 6:15') is structurally distinct from chapter 9's opening ('Dan adjusted the rearview mirror'). No style bible example lines found verbatim. All character dialogue registers match style bible profiles.
edits: Added 'and the faint bar of the stove light along the counter' to the 2 AM kitchen scene when Mira closes the refrigerator, restoring continuity with chapter 7's established stove-light motif
